description: Using GNU Octave, perform a linear regression analysis on the Auto MPG dataset (auto_mpg.csv) located in ~/Documents/datasets/. This dataset contains real fuel consumption data for 398 automobiles from the UCI Machine Learning Repository. Load the CSV data, use 'weight' (column 5) as the independent variable and 'mpg' (column 1) as the dependent variable. Filter out any rows with missing values (marked as '?'). Compute the linear regression coefficients (slope and intercept) using the normal equation or polyfit. Create a scatter plot of weight vs mpg with the regression line overlaid in red. Add axis labels 'Vehicle Weight (lbs)' and 'Miles Per Gallon (MPG)', add the title 'Auto MPG: Weight vs Fuel Efficiency', and display the R-squared value in the plot legend or as text annotation. Save the plot as '/home/ga/plots/auto_mpg_regression.png'.
